attributes.description: Insider-threat-focused view of filesystem evidence parsed by MFTECmd (Eric Zimmerman) via KAPE — both the $MFT static table AND the $J Update Sequence Number Journal. Noise-reduced: the hero panel filters $J changes to NON-system paths and high-signal reasons only (the rest is Windows servicing noise). Below: large files in user-writable locations (exfil staging signal), recently created scripts in any path (payload / LOLBin drop signal), timestomping + alternate data streams (anti-forensics), and a high-signal-only $J operations timeline. Pivots: confirm a Downloads-folder file against the Browser dashboard's downloads list; correlate a recently created .ps1 against the Prefetch / EventLog PowerShell panels to see if it actually ran.
